comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Root-caused via hypothesis-driven debugging (isolated fresh-install repro). VERDICT: environment-only artifact, NOT a source defect. On a clean venv from current origin/main (bf489e508), tests/scripts/reducer/test_managed_refs.py passes (10 passed) and full collection is clean (4316 collected, 0 errors). The imported symbols all resolve: _normalize_comment_body is defined at rebar_reconciler/outbound_comments.py:119 and re-exported by outbound_differ.py:38. The reported ModuleNotFoundError only reproduces on a worktree/editable-install predating commit 3d8be7fec (2026-06-29), which CREATED outbound_comments.py in the per-concern differ split — such a stale tree has the rebar_reconciler package (so 'import rebar_reconciler' works) but lacks the outbound_comments submodule (so the dotted import fails). Resolution: already fixed on main by that split; no behavior change needed. Added a regression guard (tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_outbound_comments_split_import.py) that imports outbound_differ and asserts the re-exported comment-split names resolve — verified teeth: renaming outbound_comments.py away reproduces the exact ModuleNotFoundError (RED), present = GREEN. This pins the split so a real future removal/rename fails loudly here instead of resurfacing as a confusing collection error.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

Importing `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py` fails at line ~38 with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'rebar_reconciler.outbound_comments'`, surfacing as a pytest COLLECTION ERROR on `tests/scripts/reducer/test_managed_refs.py` during a full-suite run. Discovered while landing the open-bugs batch (reducer-cache / linter / perf / reconcile-test fixes).

## Reproduction Steps

1. Fresh venv on current main: `python3 -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install -e '.[dev]'`.
2. `.venv/bin/pytest tests/scripts/reducer/test_managed_refs.py -q` (or a full-suite run).

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** the module imports and the test collects/runs (or skips) cleanly.
- **Actual:** `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'rebar_reconciler.outbound_comments'` raised from `outbound_differ.py`, erroring the whole test module at collection.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `import rebar_reconciler.outbound_differ` succeeds (no ModuleNotFoundError) on a fresh install from main.
- [ ] The symbols the importer needs (e.g. `_normalize_comment_body`) resolve to their real definition location; the import points at the correct module.
- [ ] `tests/scripts/reducer/test_managed_refs.py` collects and runs (passes/skips) rather than erroring at import.
- [ ] A regression test guards the import/collection so the breakage cannot silently recur.
- [ ] No regression in `tests/scripts/reducer/`.
compiled_state.title: outbound_differ imports a missing rebar_reconciler.outbound_comments module (collection error in test_managed_refs)
